name: Allied Cash Advance
description_short: Allied Cash Advance is a short-term consumer lender with ~230 retail storefronts offering payday loans ($100–$865), installment loans, check cashing, and ancillary financial services.
description_long:
Allied Cash Advance was founded in 1999 and is headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio (7755 Montgomery Rd, Suite 500). It operates as a subsidiary of Allied Cash Holdings, running approximately 230 physical retail branches across roughly five states. The company is a member of the Community Financial Services Association of America (CFSA), an industry trade group that sets voluntary best-practice standards for short-term lenders, and holds an A– rating from the Cincinnati BBB — though it is not BBB accredited. It is NMLS-registered through its prepaid card partner Ouro Global, Inc. (NMLS #932678) and holds state lending licenses in the jurisdictions where it operates.

Allied Cash Advance's core offering is small-dollar consumer lending. Payday (cash advance) loans range from approximately $100 to $865, with fees of $10–$30 per $100 borrowed on a two-week term — translating to APRs that can reach roughly 400%. Installment loans extend up to approximately $5,000 with monthly maintenance fees ranging from $25 to $134.07. Beyond lending, in-store branches offer check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, Netspend prepaid Mastercard accounts (via authorized delegate Ouro Global), prepaid phones, calling cards, gift cards, and in-store tax return preparation and filing. Loans are intended for consumers with limited or no access to traditional bank credit.

A notable differentiator is speed: in-store loan applicants can receive funds deposited to a debit card in as little as 30 minutes. The company offers Auto Pay enrollment that aligns payment due dates to a borrower's pay cycle — biweekly, semi-monthly, or monthly — which can reduce the risk of missed payments. Allied Cash also provides Pocket360, an online account portal at application.alliedcash.com, where borrowers can manage their loan, check refinancing eligibility, and track payment history without visiting a store. With 25+ years of operation and a physical retail footprint, it offers face-to-face service that purely online lenders cannot match.

Honestly assessed, Allied Cash Advance fills a real gap for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need fast, small-dollar cash and lack access to traditional credit — but it does so at very high cost. APRs approaching 400% on payday loans can trap borrowers in debt cycles if loans are rolled over repeatedly. The company does not prominently display interest rates or APR figures on its website before application, making true cost comparison difficult. Online applications are not processed directly through Allied Cash but are instead routed to Check 'n Go, meaning the company is not itself an online lender. Geographic availability is limited to approximately five states, and no dedicated mobile app has been confirmed. For consumers who can qualify for a credit union payday alternative loan (PAL) or a CDFI product at rates below 36% APR, those options are significantly cheaper.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee applicable to loan products. Contact provider for details.

tiers.name: Payday / Cash Advance Loan
features: Funds deposited to debit card in as little as 30 minutes in-store
Auto Pay enrollment available synced to your pay cycle

tiers.name: Installment Loan
features: Monthly maintenance fees ranging from approximately $25 to $134.07
Refinancing eligibility checkable via Pocket360 portal

pros: In-store loan funding in as little as 30 minutes directly to a debit card
Founded in 1999 — 25+ years of short-term consumer lending experience
Approximately 230 retail storefronts offering face-to-face service
CFSA membership signals adherence to voluntary industry best-practice standards
Pocket360 online portal allows loan management and refinancing eligibility checks without visiting a store
Auto Pay enrollment syncs payment due dates to borrower's actual pay cycle (biweekly, semi-monthly, or monthly)
Wide ancillary services in-store: check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, tax prep, prepaid cards
cons: Payday loan APRs can reach approximately 400% — among the highest-cost borrowing options available
Interest rates and APR not prominently displayed on website prior to application, hindering cost comparison
Not a direct online lender — web applications are routed to Check 'n Go rather than processed by Allied Cash itself
Geographic availability limited to approximately 5 states — most U.S. consumers cannot access branches
No confirmed dedicated mobile app for loan management on the go
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need same-day emergency cash and lack access to traditional bank credit
Borrowers who prefer in-person service and live near one of Allied Cash's ~230 retail storefronts
Consumers who need small-dollar funds (under $865) within the hour and have a debit card for deposit
People seeking ancillary financial services (check cashing, money orders, tax prep) alongside a short-term loan
diagnosis: Allied Cash Advance is best suited for unbanked or underbanked consumers in its operating states who need fast, small-dollar emergency cash and have no viable alternative — such as a credit card, personal loan, or credit union PAL. The main caveat is cost: payday loan APRs approaching 400% make this one of the most expensive borrowing options available, and borrowers who roll over loans risk a debt cycle that far exceeds the original loan amount.
services: Netspend prepaid Mastercard (via Ouro Global, Inc., NMLS #932678)
In-store tax return preparation and filing
Pocket360 online account portal (loan management, refinancing eligibility)
Auto Pay enrollment for loan repayment
